videos.title: The Big Lesson of the 2017 ICO Boom (and Why It Matters Now)
videos.text: so a lot of people are talking about stos and securitized tokens in general being super interesting I think maybe almost everyone I think missed the big lessons in the 2017 IC over the big lesson comes from this question why on earth were entrepreneurs willing to issue completely unregulated asset one that many of them didn't even really understand that was an existential regulatory risk to their company and on the other side why were households and retail investors willing to buy it I think what that really is demonstrating is that a bunch of really well-intentioned regulation to protect investors has made it functionally impossible for any kind of households access high-value deals especially at a time when interest rates are super low where equity valuations are already insanely high where do you go to buy yield particularly as a retail investor as an institution maybe or as a massive in credit investor you can go to private markets where this higher yield and the private markets can move down the risk chain by buying you know earlier and earlier stage startups where they're even doing larger seed rounds now but for the entrepreneur now if it's gonna be prohibitively expensive for them to actually able to access public markets by now is basically there is a functional cartel of accounting firms law firms and investment banks that's protects access to public markets because of all the regulatory burden and then even if you do get through all those you hire the white shoe law firm you hire the big four accounting firm you get underwritten then once you do it you have to be large enough company that you can afford to fulfill all the reporting obligations on going into the future for an early stage entrepreneur there is no real access to public markets there so are the central doors are set forth to private market problems of private markets they're very relationship driven they're extremely inefficient because there's so few people involved in them and because you're selling big chunks of your company at a time you're selling additional upside because you're always one preferred stock and you're giving up a ton of control over your future developments on the retail side these people were willing to buy us that they no understanding of because they don't have access to those high-value deals are happening now primarily in private markets whether it be through PVC will whatever the real thing that was demonstrated there is that there's too much of a barrier for public markets to access early-stage entrepreneurs that's the real lesson of the 2017 massive demand for icos now how does that apply to sto a peep I'm really excited about right now our sto is a thing that can potentially make a new way around that and the answer to that is it's really to be seen it really depends on how the sec approaches it yes C can approach it by saying that tokenizer carries our securities but in some of these venues we're going to allow a little bit of a sandbox from experimentation or they can be pretty rigid about it in which case it's going to make no difference doing that for some versus paper equities the other particularly interesting about being able to settle on a blockchain versus being settling in a state registrar or something you can already do and all you're doing is replacing a settlement layer but in the world where the SEC maybe allows for those tokenized securities exchange that organizes reg D or reg a sales of tokenized securities and then after a certain period of time those things become liquidly tradable unless you incur the reporting obligations that come with having too many people on your cap table now there's already solution potentially in place for that problem which is that companies like each raid that sell there's some particular word for you might be like beneficiary ownership or whatever but aren't technically on the cap table if you look at Facebook there's some insanely low like only a few thousand people who are technically on the cap table I'm not sure exactly what the number is because if you hold the exposure for something like e trade you don't necessarily count as being on the cap table you just have exposure to that equities value so whatever the infrastructure is allowing for that thing maybe you could have an exchange where the exchange is the only person typing on the cap table but the exposure to the equity is liquidly accessible by retail traders now that is so obviously a structure like that that's kind of using a bunch of half exceptions in order to create something that can be liquidly traded but isn't technically having a ton of people on the cap table so obviously against the spirit of the law maybe okay according to the letter of the law but what's the SEC going to do with that are they going to actually enforce it or they're going to use some plugs coming like that if that infrastructure gets built as an opportunity to experiment with a reasonably small portion of population that isn't going to screw over a huge portion of their you know suppose it constituents and so basically whether or not there's anything interesting happening with access to capital through stos has entirely to do with how rigid the SEC decides to be about whether these can be treated a little bit differently and a little more softly than its curious thank you for watching if you have any questions left let us know we'll make more videos to explain and make sure to sign up for a free beta tiger trading com

videos.title: The Crypto Boom of 2017: Bitcoin Hits $20K and ICO Mania Explodes
videos.text: welcome to Once Upon a coin documenting the ongoing history of blockchain and cryptocurrency and as always I'm your Storyteller Jason Cassidy today's story is a magical one and something that you were definitely privileged to have lived through and experienced it's a story of perseverance Vindication and a whole lot of cool stuff all bound together in a cryptographic rapper this is a story of crypto in 2017 [Music] sun cast a shadow a digital line is coming over the mountain and over your mind downin one block at a time moving into the future one block at a time in the previous episode in our review of cryptocurrency and blockchain over the years the industry was in a pretty bad spot 2016 saw the extension of a brutal bare Market the Dow badly blew up and pretty much changed the fate of ethereum forever while Bitcoin saw itself engulfed in the next installment of this Toshi Nakamoto identity drama it's a heck of an episode and I highly encourage you check check it out before you go too deep down into the 2017 rabbit hole with me here but mostly it was Bitcoin that hurt the most having to away down in the100 the $200 USD range for seemingly forever and while it wasn't exactly Forever by the time that 2016 had made its presence felt it meant that cryptonauts had now suffered through a nasty threeyear long crypto winter but near the end of the year Bitcoin began to show signs of Life breaking out and getting into the $900 USD range as December came to a close and of course now everyone is wondering what would 2017 bring well you're about to find out it all started with the measure of good news something the industry could desperately use by now the price of Bitcoin hit the $1,000 per coin Mark right at the beginning of January and this was significant because it had not seen that price level previously for over 1,000 consecutive days what came next was a rapid fire succession of historically relevant developments mixed in with some bull market Euphoria we've actually reached the point in crypto's history where so much stuff is happening on a daily and weekly basis that to fully document everything that was happening in 2017 from here on out would take days not hours so let's take a look at some of the most important events because what was about to take place in the blockchain world would impact it in ways that no one was prepared for in February the ethereum Enterprise Alliance was launched boasting some pretty big founding members such as JP Morgan micro Microsoft and Intel despite the setback caused by the Dows implosion a year prior ethereum was still making big moves and if you're paying close attention around this time then you notice that acts like the formation of the Enterprise Alliance were one of the catalysts to help Propel ethereum into the number two spot behind Bitcoin to show how widely successful this initiative was in getting Enterprise level players involved and thus invested into ethereum just 6 months after the formation of the alliance it had grown to boast over 50 members wow as big as this news was for ethereum something much much bigger was about to happen for them something that would help cement their status as one of the true legendary blockchain networks but before ethereum could take the spotlight fully it first had to share it with Bitcoin for a little while because in March news came out that Bitcoin was having a little bit of trouble when it came to ETFs or exchange traded funds they are being denied on mass by the SEC or Securities Exchange Commission the winkl Vos Twins were the most noteworthy of all the applicants that were denied in 2017 around getting a Bitcoin ETF off the ground however this desired to have investment funds for Bitcoin would not simply go away there was sever more attempts to get it off the ground in the US taking place in the years that followed the sec's denial of the ETFs were based upon the review that most of the cryptocurrency industry was still unregulated and then from their perspective it just made no sense to usher in a formal investment vehicle given the current climate basically the industry was just going to have to mature a lot more before this type of offering was going to be greenlighted on April 1st something that was most definitely not an April Fool's joke took place but I'm sure at least a few people thought it was the Japanese government formally recognized Bitcoin as a legal payment method by codifying it in law this was yet another important step towards legitimacy for the nasan cryptocurrency as soon as the calter had flipped over to May things started to change Bitcoin began to move up very fast at a rate of sever hundred US per day and it ended the month at over $2,000 a coin this was a setup for what would soon become the next great bull market in crypto the month of June helped Propel the market in this direction as it saw a lot of new projects introduce himself via crowd sales one such example of this was eos who conducted an Ico or initial coin offering EOS had a unique take on the traditional Ico model and instead decided to run theirs for almost a full calendar year including it on June 1st 2018 the sale was incredibly successful and the project raised a total of 4.1 billion usar yeah that's just nuts to me and I'm used to big numbers in crypto as big as the EOS Ico was it was just one of many top projects that were debuting around this time using the Ico model most of these big projects were making use of the erc20 token standard and debuting their currencies on ethereum projects like bankor 10x and bra all chose to build on ethereum and Ico their tokens there pulling in some huge numbers in the process 10x raised 80 million in just 7 minutes while Bor amassed a war chest of $153 million from investors who were all too eager to get their hands on the hottest new token on the Block while the icos just kept coming Fast and Furious throughout the summer in July was actually an exchange that made big news and this wasn't just some shitcoin exchange either no no no no this was an exchange that would go on to become the king of crypto just a few short years later enter bance on July 14th bance officially opened their virtual doors to customers around the world and invited them all to buy and sell crypto to their heart's content it was a successful introduction into the industry for the young exchange and in the coming years bance would go on to grow its reach and influence until it could be felt in nearly every corner of the blockchain before the summer was over though a disturbance in the forest could be felt by everyone who was paying attention to bitcoin the block siiz debate had evolved over the years starting out as a practice and intellectual banter with the goal of making Bitcoin the very best it could be but with so much money at stake around the direction that Bitcoin was going to be taking political motivations along with personal ones tend to get involved Bitcoin was no longer just a hobbyist curiosity like it started odas it was now truly looking like the future of Finance it also meant that some big existing Financial players like Western Union PayPal and even Banks had to start taking cryptocurrency more seriously now as a potentially disruptive force in the not so distant future all these realities eventually came together to create a movement that pushed the blocksize debate into full out blocksize War half of the community within Bitcoin firmly believed that small blocks were the better path to take long term the other half of the community felt that satoshi's vision for Bitcoin as lat in the white paper should be followed loyally being treated like an owner's man ual in some cases they felt that since following the white paper so far had worked exactly as Satoshi said it would then the choice was clear they wanted to allow the block size to grow in lock step with the demand for Block space with more transactions on bitcoin bringing more Prosperity with no resolution in sight and both camps now firmly digging their heels in the blockchain engaged in the only way it knows how to resolve such a stalemate on August 1st Bitcoin experienced a contentious hard Fork as essentially creating a new copy of itself there were now two versions of Bitcoin one version would maintain the small blocks and would also retain the BTC ticker symbol the other version which would have bigger blocks and would more closely follow satoshi's white paper of Bitcoin being peer-to-peer electronic cash was called Bitcoin cash and its ticker symbol became bch which Camp got which Bitcoin name in ticker symbol became more of a political process than it was a tech technical or even legal one both the miners and the exchanges played a huge role in both versions of Bitcoin surviving the fork and having a future while competing against each other some of the notable figures behind this blowup were Adam back who represented the BTC side and Roger ve who represented the Bitcoin cash side all this drama had no impact on the upper trajectory of Bitcoin BTC though as it finished the month of August trading in the $4,500 range before the month was over though another entr into the blockchain space showed up and made a little bit of noise on August 30th Tron a blockchain network headed up by Justin Sun conducted its Ico over the course of several days and was able to raise an impressive $7 million us just a month later in the industry got more good news with the formalization of the erc20 token standard on ethereum which took place on September 11th Ico minion was already well underway by now but the formalization was still important as it pretty much meant that when it came to ethereum all tokens would be adhering to the same standard this was Paramount to the Future adoption of ethereum as a meant it would be very easy to build on from a developer's perspective and very easy to use from a consumer's perspective in September it was another top smart contract based blockchain's turn to take the spotlight and it came in the form of the cardano project the network launched on September 27th touting itself as an alternative to ethereum there was a reason to believe that cardano may in fact have a chance at offering more than ethereum could and that's because The Man Behind cardano Charles hoskinson was a co-founder of ethereum and thus knew the network and its weaknesses at a level that most simply could not comprehend by the time October hit Bitcoin was soaring reaching $5,000 and then moving up to $6,000 per coin before month's end it was official Bitcoin and everything else that was being dragged along with it was now in a Raging Bull Market euphoria began to kick in as the currency just kept making new all-time highs speculation was rampant would Bitcoin hit $ 10,000 in this cycle what about 50 or $100,000 some were even suggesting that this was the beginning of A1 million us per coin Bitcoin Ascension look everyone has a dream and for some bitcoiners in 2017 this was theirs November saw even more price increases with Bitcoin breaching the 10,000 us dollar Mark for the very first time this was New Territory considering the psychological barrier of five figures now being utterly smashed doubters and even haters of Bitcoin were now forced to ask themselves the difficult and painful question of did I get it wrong here by December the crypto world was on fire one that seemed like it couldn't be put out even if the entire Atlantic Ocean was dumped onto it big news was coming out too which just acted as an accelerate to the wild nature of the market to fully appreciate what this was like you really had to live through it it's just an experience you simply will never ever forget to give you an idea of what an acid trip December 2017 was here's a snippet crypto kitties an nft or non-fungible token project built on ethereum saw a flurry of a trading activity that made its creators Dapper Labs an overnight success the project Drew so much attention that the ethereum network couldn't handle it sending gas fees to insane prices it was very difficult and very very expensive to get a transaction through on the ethereum network during this time what we were seeing here was an appetite beginning to form for nfts now that ethereum had a token standard to support them which was the ERC 721 in the future the next great build Market would be propelled by nfts but they would need several more years of development before they were ready to take Center Stage another sign of the detached from reality type of energy that was flowing around this time can be seen with the following Farmingdale a new york-based Beverage Company decided they would capitalize on the hype when they announced they were changing their Flagship product name from Long Island IC to Simply long blockchain Corp the stock immediately shut up 200% validating that if you were even remotely close to anything to do with blockchain around this time you could basically print money this roller coaster ride hit its apex on December 19th when the price of Bitcoin hit its high of 19,7 183 us dollar coming oh so close to the $20,000 us Milestone the closing to what was truly a surreal year saw the price settle back down a little bit into the $16,000 range everyone was on a high right now given that if you had held Bitcoin during the brutal bear Market you were now flush with purchasing power giving the crazy gains experience in such a short time as the sun was finally setting on 2017 one thing was becoming clear to those that were paying close attention crypto was about to enter into a new epoch heading into 2018 one that would see new faces technology and events that would rise up to play dominant roles nfts defi stable coins smart contract based blockchains in the metaverse all these Innovations were now ready for prime time but their Journey to the upper echelon of crypto wasn't going to be easy it wasn't going to be easy at all with many of the challenges that they would face creating some of the most entertaining and notorious events in digital currency history and that's what we're going to be looking at next episode so if you want to know the scoop then you better check out the story of crypto in 2018 I hope you enjoyed today's stroll down memory lane until next time have fun and enjoy life my friends I'll be back soon with another incredible story to tell you
